title: Skill improves accuracy in the identifiable benchmark
claim: Blended skill + stake weighting reduces forecast error relative to equal weights when the DGP has a latent truth.
interpretation: The online skill layer tracks intrinsic forecaster quality well in the latent-truth setting, so up-weighting better forecasters improves the aggregate. This 21% figure is from the synthetic benchmark (latent_fixed DGP, N=10, default parameters). On real Elia wind data with tuned parameters (γ=16, ρ=0.5) and 7 forecasters, the improvement reaches 33.7% over equal weights. The size of the improvement also depends on the deposit policy: fixed deposits (which isolate the skill signal) yield the full synthetic advantage, while bankroll-fraction deposits reduce it to ~5%.
caveat: This is a clean benchmark with identifiable truth, not a strategic or adversarial setting.
limitations: Does not hold with small panels (N ≤ 3) where estimation noise dominates
Advantage shrinks under bankroll-fraction deposit policy (~5% vs 21%)
Requires heterogeneous forecaster skill; homogeneous panels see no benefit
Short horizons (T < 50) may not allow EWMA to converge

title: Skill estimates recover after perturbation
claim: The EWMA skill layer recovers correct ordering within ~50 rounds after a shock to all skill weights.
interpretation: The exponential decay parameter γ controls the speed-accuracy trade-off: faster decay recovers quicker but is noisier.
caveat: Recovery speed depends on γ, signal-to-noise ratio, and the number of active agents.
limitations: Recovery time increases with panel size (more agents to re-rank)
Under high noise (low signal-to-noise ratio), recovery may be incomplete
Non-stationary skill (continuous drift) prevents full convergence

title: Wealth concentration stays controlled
claim: Gini and HHI do not rise sharply under skill + stake weighting compared to equal weights.
interpretation: Skill gating attenuates noisy large bets without concentrating wealth in few agents.
caveat: Under some deposit policies (e.g. wealth-fraction), concentration can still rise if skilled agents compound returns.
limitations: Wealth-fraction deposit policy can increase concentration via compounding
Very long horizons (T >> 1000) may show slow concentration drift
Highly skilled outlier agents can still accumulate disproportionate wealth

title: Calibration holds in the centre, weakens in the tails
claim: The aggregate forecast is well-calibrated for central quantiles but PIT coverage degrades for extreme quantiles.
metric: PIT coverage gap < 5% for central, > 10% for tails
interpretation: The linear pool preserves calibration for moderate probability events but under-disperses in the tails. Note: the baseline DGP produces miscalibrated forecasts by construction (uniform noise on quantiles). The latent-fixed DGP was introduced to provide a cleaner calibration benchmark.
caveat: Tail miscalibration is the main modelling limitation and is shared across all weighting methods. Results should be interpreted relative to the DGP used.
limitations: Tail miscalibration is inherent to the linear pool and shared across all methods
Baseline DGP produces miscalibrated forecasts by construction
Only tested on latent_fixed DGP; other DGPs may show different calibration patterns
Small panels may not provide enough diversity for good calibration

title: Equal weighting is a strong baseline
claim: Uniform weights are surprisingly hard to beat, especially under non-stationarity or small panels.
interpretation: Blended skill × stake wins when the panel includes forecasters with heterogeneous skill and enough rounds for the EWMA to converge (typically >50). Under non-stationarity, regime shifts reset the skill estimates and equal weighting dominates during re-learning. With small panels (N ≤ 3), estimation noise in σ can erase the advantage entirely.
caveat: With few agents or short horizons, estimation noise in σ can erase the advantage.
limitations: Small panels (N ≤ 3): estimation noise erases the advantage
Short horizons (T < 50): EWMA has not converged
Non-stationarity: regime shifts reset skill estimates, equal dominates during re-learning
Homogeneous skill: no signal to exploit, equal is optimal
